summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Strategic Program Manager who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing processes grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can optimize operational work—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Conduct focus groups and 1:1 interviews with an NGO leadership team to surface decision-making and accountability gaps
Manage 20+ {prog|programs} and 10+ vendors, refining {prog|program} positioning, delivery strategy, and operations via {data|feedback analysis}
Optimize {cross|cross-functional} processes for 70+ staff, creating comprehensive documentation that reduced onboarding time by 75%
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders} to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, boosting {prog|program} understanding and achieving a 78% program enrollment rate
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized campus recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite executives at top universities to attract talent

jd.meta: Kelly Talent Supply Chain Consultant — Hillsboro, OR (Hybrid). VMS tool admin, talent data reporting, PMO-Direct Sourcing liaison, sales support, onboarding audits. Required: 2+ yr program/client mgmt + procurement tech; VMS tools (Fieldglass/IQN/Beeline).

changeLog.note: Initial build for Kelly Talent Supply Chain Consultant. Role type: Operations/PM with data reporting emphasis. Summary: Strategic Program Manager. ODN: Pair B (diagnostic/redesign story). InGenius: Vendor/PM Set (manage 20+, optimize cross-functional, needs assessments, webinars). Vestas: onboarding-first ops variant. NextGen/Kronos: standard 4 bullets. Fit flag: partial — program management + data reporting match; VMS/supply-chain domain does not.
